t_ch: 喔，对了！我也给老爹准备了礼物，你快打开看看吧！
t_en: Oh, yeah! I got a gift for you, too! Open it and take a look!

t_ch: 宝、宝贝女儿送给我的？那可得好好看看，好好看看……
t_en: For me? I'm curious...

t_ch: 打开了虎克送给费斯曼的生日礼物盒……
t_en: Fersman opens the present that Hook got him...

t_ch: 这是…「地髓矿脉探针」？！
t_en: It's... the Geomarrow Probe!?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，这是我和{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}在拍卖会上买下的探针！竞价用的钱就是卖掉「菌菌」才拿到的哦！
t_en: Hehe, {NICKNAME} helped me to bid for it at the auction! I bought it with the money I got from selling Junjun!

t_ch: 有了它，老爹肯定就能把「矿脉探测器」修好了吧？
t_en: With this, you should be able to repair the vein detector, right daddy?

t_ch: …老爹？
t_en: ...Daddy?

t_ch: 谢谢你，虎克！这件生日礼物…老爹特别喜欢，只是现在可能还用不上……
t_en: Thank you so much, Hook! Daddy really likes this birthday gift... but I might not be able to use it right now...

t_ch: …哎？现在用不上吗？
t_en: ...Huh? Why not?

t_ch: 虎克，你听我说……
t_en: It's like this, Hook...

t_ch: 这些玩偶…就是老爹卖掉了「矿脉探测器」以后才给你买的。
t_en: I... bought your gift with the money I got from selling the vein detector.

t_ch: …啊！那虎克的礼物不是也…派不上用场了？
t_en: ...Ah!? So my gift is also... useless...

t_ch: 虎克…你喜欢老爹送你的礼物吗？
t_en: Hook... do you still like the gift I got for you?

t_ch: …当、当然啦！老爹送给虎克的礼物，是世界上最棒的礼物！
t_en: ...O—Of course! Your gifts are the best in the world!

t_ch: 老爹也这么觉得，虎克…老爹也这么觉得。
t_en: I feel the same way about your gift, Hook. Me and you are in the same boat.

t_ch: 老爹！呜——
t_en: Daddy! *sob*

t_ch: 你买下「矿脉探测器」了？
t_en: Did you buy the vein detector?

t_ch: 哦？你知道的还不少。我也不装了，没错，的确是费斯曼那老头卖给我的。
t_en: Oh-ho? Did a little bird tell you? Alright, I won't pretend otherwise. Yes, old man Fersman sold it to me.

t_ch: 可惜没能搞来地髓探针…光有这探测器有啥用啊，唉。
t_en: Sadly, I wasn't able to get hold of the Geomarrow Probe... *sigh* What good is this detector on its own?

t_ch: 小偷是你安排的？
t_en: Did you send the thief to steal the detector?

t_ch: 小偷？我可没有安排过什么小偷。
t_en: Thief? I didn't send a thief.

t_ch: 不过，我倒是有安排一个小学徒去参加拍卖，遗憾失手了。
t_en: I did arrange for a young apprentice to attend the auction, but he lost out in the bidding.

t_ch: 你没参加拍卖会吗？
t_en: You didn't attend the auction?

t_ch: 我安排了一个小学徒去参加拍卖，遗憾失手了。
t_en: I arranged for a young apprentice to attend the auction, but he lost out in the bidding.

t_ch: 你不就是收废品的吗？
t_en: Aren't you a scrap collector?

t_ch: 哼，你根本不明白古代遗物的价值。
t_en: Hmph, clearly you don't understand the value of ancient relics.

t_ch: 可惜，现在再想找到一枚地髓探针可太不容易了…这笔生意真是越想越亏，哎……
t_en: Shame, it's becoming increasingly difficult to find a Geomarrow Probe... The more I think about it, the more I realize that this deal was a big mistake... *sigh*

t_ch: 干脆把它卖给费斯曼吧。
t_en: Why not just sell it to Fersman?

t_ch: 费斯曼也不会想当这个冤大头吧？不过我可以去问问，能出手的话还是尽早出了才好……
t_en: Fersman wouldn't want to be the sucker, would he? But maybe I should ask him... It would be great to get rid of it as early as possible...

t_ch: 费斯曼有地髓探针。
t_en: Fersman has the Geomarrow Probe.

t_ch: …真的？
t_en: ...Really?

t_ch: 哼，那我就姑且信你…等下我去找费斯曼，问问他探针打算怎么卖。
t_en: Hmph, I'll give you the benefit of the doubt... Let me find Fersman and ask if he intends to sell the probe.

t_ch: 你再等等，总有一天会找到的。
t_en: Maybe one day you'll find it.

t_ch: 这探针有多稀有我会不知道？我得查查拍卖会上那个买家到底是谁……
t_en: Do you think I don't know how rare that probe is? I need to find out who that buyer at the auction was...

t_ch: 科鲁泽的信件和包裹
t_en: Kluzer's letter and parcel

t_ch: 矿镐和安全帽
t_en: Mechanical Mining Pick and safety helmet

t_ch: 外来…外来者…克拉拉…的朋友。
t_en: Outssssiders... F—Friends of Clara.

t_ch: 你好，帕金斯！
t_en: Hey Perkins!

t_ch: 你是怎么靠肉眼辨认机器人的……
t_en: How did you know that was Perkins...

t_ch: 克拉拉把你修好了呀？
t_en: Did Clara fix you?

t_ch: 谢…谢谢…外来者…救了…克拉拉。
t_en: Th—Th—Th—Thank you... for rescuing... Claaaara.

t_ch: 这小家伙说话怎么磕磕巴巴的啊？
t_en: Seems like Perkins picked up a stammer?

t_ch: 大概是语言模块出了些什么故障吧…你能带我们去见克拉拉吗？
t_en: Its language module must have malfunctioned... Can you take us to Clara?

t_ch: 外来…是…克拉拉的朋友…跟跟跟…跟我来……
t_en: Outsiders... are f—friends of Clara... Follow me...

t_ch: 访客来了，克拉拉。
t_en: You have visitors, Clara.

t_ch: 啊，是「变量」哥哥姐姐们！
t_en: Ah! It's the variables!

t_ch: 奇怪的绰号增加了…
t_en: Another nickname for the collection...

t_ch: 那他一定是「常量」史瓦罗了。
t_en: Nice haircut, Svarog.

t_ch: 你又想挨打了是不是…
t_en: Are you trying to start another fight...

t_ch: 刚才…克拉拉感觉到了很大的震动，似乎是从上面传下来的。史瓦罗先生说，那大概率是…是……
t_en: There was a huge shaking just now coming up through the ground. Mr. Svarog said it... must have been...

t_ch: 计算重启…结论——「造物引擎」，可能性：<unbreak>97.66%</unbreak>。
t_en: Recalculating... Conclusion: Engine of Creation. <unbreak>97.66%</unbreak> probability.

t_ch: 「造物引擎」是你的兄弟吗？
t_en: Old friend of yours?

t_ch: 你不早提醒我们还有那种兵器…
t_en: Any other secret weapons you'd like to tell us about...?

t_ch: 「造物引擎」是曾经的筑城者修筑的巨型机械。它并非兵器。
t_en: The Engine of Creation was an immense construction machine created by the Architects. It was not built to fight.

t_ch: 行吧，反正我们赢了…多亏了那台大机器人，我们终于把「星核」给解决咯。布洛妮娅也给出了承诺，上下层的封锁很快会解除啦。
t_en: Well whatever, we won... Thanks to that giant robot, we were finally able to take care of the Stellaron... and Bronya made a promise — the Underworld and the Overworld will soon be reunited.

t_ch: 这样一来，史瓦罗就不用…呃，暂时不用…为下层区的「存护」发愁了吧？
t_en: Which means that you, Svarog — uh... You won't have to worry about preserving the Underworld... for the time being.

t_ch: 谢谢三月姐姐…谢谢大家！老实说，克拉拉一开始没有抱太大的希望……
t_en: March, everyone... thank you so much! I wasn't sure you guys would be able to do it, but...

t_ch: 但是现在…史瓦罗先生，可以吗？
t_en: But now... Mr. Svarog, what do you say?

t_ch: 哥哥姐姐们说过，你们是来自其它星球的旅行者，对吗？克拉拉也想成为和你们一样的人——总有一天…克拉拉也想去更远的地方旅行，结识更多的朋友。
t_en: You guys said you were travelers from other worlds, right? I want to be just like you... One day, I'm gonna go traveling to faraway places and make new friends.

t_ch: 愿望一定会实现的。
t_en: I know you will.

t_ch: 未来都在你的手中！
t_en: Your future is yours to decide!

t_ch: 计算结果…更新。任务编号<unbreak>0001</unbreak>，「存护下层区」——完成。任务关闭。
t_en: Calculation result... update. Mission number <unbreak>0001</unbreak> — Preserve the Underworld — complete.

t_ch: 不愧是布洛妮娅，真是雷厉风行呀！
t_en: That's our Bronya — quick as a flash!

t_ch: 确实和一般的拖延症患者有所差别。
t_en: She certainly moves a lot faster than some people I know...

t_ch: …不许拉踩！
t_en: ...If you haven't got anything nice to say right now, zip it!

t_ch: 那个小偷…怎么又来偷零件了呀？不知道这次有没有留下什么线索……
t_en: The thief has stolen parts again? How am I going to track down this crook?

t_ch: 克拉拉，你在烦恼什么？
t_en: Clara, what's wrong?

t_ch: 啊，是外来者{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}……！
t_en: Oh, it's you!

t_ch: 那个，其实是这样…营地里这些天发生了几起奇怪的失窃事件。
t_en: There have been a few unusual thefts in the dwelling over the past few days.

t_ch: 流浪者叔叔阿姨们说，大家最近辛苦收集来的零件总是莫名其妙地消失。他们怀疑营地里可能出了小偷——
t_en: The vagrants say that the parts they worked so hard to obtain have completely vanished. They're worried that there's a thief in the camp...

t_ch: 可大家搜查了很久，也没有揪出偷走零件的小偷。更奇怪的是，那个小偷似乎只偷零件，对其他贵重物品根本不感兴趣……
t_en: Despite our investigations, no one has been able to track down the thief. What's even stranger is that the thief appears to be only interested in stealing parts, and doesn't care about other valuable items...

t_ch: 叔叔阿姨们实在没办法，就来拜托史瓦罗先生调查这件事，但暂时也没查出任何结果…克拉拉自己也小小地调查了一下，还是没发现什么有用的线索……
t_en: The vagrants had no choice but to ask Mr. Svarog to look into it, but he still hasn't found anything... I investigated the matter myself, but I came up empty-handed...

t_ch: 啊…克拉拉感觉，有些…头晕。怎么会有这么奇怪的小偷……
t_en: Jeez... I'm baffled. What a strange thief...

t_ch: 一起把线索从头捋一遍吧。
t_en: Don't worry. We can go over the clues together.

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}也要来调查这件事吗？这样的话，或许能发现克拉拉没注意到的线索……
t_en: Are you also here to look into this case, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}? Perhaps if we work together we can uncover some clues that I missed...

t_ch: 只是…其实克拉拉掌握的信息也不多。小偷很狡猾，几乎没在营地里留下痕迹……
t_en: It's just... I haven't found enough clues yet. The thief is really tricky. They haven't left any traces in the dwelling...

t_ch: 不如我们再去现场调查一下？
t_en: Why don't we go back to the crime scene and start from there?

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}也要来调查这件事吗？这样的话，或许能发现克拉拉没注意到的线索……
t_en: Are you also here to look into this case, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}? Perhaps if we work together we can uncover some clues that I missed...

t_ch: 是犯罪的气息…我兴奋起来了！
t_en: Mmm! The smell of crime... How exciting!

t_ch: …{F#大、大姐姐}{M#大、大哥哥}的表情，突然有点吓人……
t_en: ...Th—That look on your face is a bit scary...

t_ch: 不过，如果你愿意帮忙调查的话…或许能发现克拉拉没注意到的线索。
t_en: But if you're willing to help look into this case, perhaps we can uncover some clues that I missed.

t_ch: 啊…对了！听说昨晚守夜巡逻的叔叔阿姨又撞到了那个小偷，但克拉拉还没来得及去问…要一起去问问看吗？
t_en: Oh! I heard the vagrants on patrol last night ran across the thief again, but I haven't had time to look into it... Should we go ask them?

t_ch: 第一手证词可是非常宝贵的。
t_en: Witnesses' firsthand accounts are crucial.

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}的台词…很帅气呢，很像在史瓦罗先生的「电影」里看到的那种人…是叫「侦探」，对吧？
t_en: You sound so cool, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}... You're like one of the characters from Mr. Svarog's movies. Aren't they called detectives?

t_ch: 看到{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}这么有干劲，克拉拉好像也激动起来了——那我们就出发吧。
t_en: Seeing you so fired up has me excited, too. Now, let's get started.

t_ch: 天才侦探少女，出发！
t_en: Genius detective girl, move out!

t_ch: 好奇怪的外号呀……
t_en: That sounds kinda weird...

t_ch: 不过…克拉拉觉得也很帅气！那我们就出发吧，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！
t_en: But actually... I think it's cool, too! Now, let's get started, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}!

t_ch: 您还好吗，叔叔？听说您昨晚撞见了那个零件小偷…可以跟我们说说昨天夜里发生的事吗？
t_en: Sir, how are you? I heard you saw the thief who stole the parts last night. Can you tell us what happened?

t_ch: 我、啊…不是，谁、谁在那里？！
t_en: Ah, wh—who's there!?

t_ch: 啊、啊！原、原来是克拉拉，我还以为是…吓死我了！
t_en: Oh! Clara, it's you! I thought it was... You scared the living daylights out of me!

t_ch: 我、我昨晚是看到那个小偷了…不过，你旁边这位是？
t_en: I—I did see the thief last night, but... who is this person next to you?

t_ch: 只是一位热心的开拓者。
t_en: I'm just a passionate trailblazer.

t_ch: 开拓者…啊，我知道了——你就是那位轰动全城的「外来者」，对吧？
t_en: Trailblazer... Oh, I know. You're the outsider who's made a name for themself around town, right?

t_ch: 哎哟、哎哟，有这样的大能人来抓小偷，那我可安心了…让、让我组织一下语言，把昨晚的经历告诉你，你等下……
t_en: Oh, wow. I feel way better knowing that a big shot like you is here to catch the thief... H—Hold on, I'll explain what happened last night. Just let me gather my thoughts first...

t_ch: 我是「地火」派来解决事件的。
t_en: The Wildfire has assigned me to solve the case.

t_ch: 喔、喔喔！地、「地火」竟然派人来了吗…这可太好了！
t_en: Oh! W—Wildfire has sent someone... That's fantastic!

t_ch: 你、你等会啊…让、让我组织一下语言，把昨晚的经历告诉你……
t_en: H—Hold on... I'll explain what happened last night. Just let me gather my thoughts first...

t_ch: 我是天才侦探少女的天才助手。
t_en: I'm the brilliant sidekick of this genius detective.

t_ch: …{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！这个外号果然还是太……
t_en: Em... This nickname is a bit embarrassing...

t_ch: 侦、侦探？我知道了，你、你是来帮忙抓小偷的，对吧？
t_en: D—Detective? I see. You're here to help catch the thief, right?

t_ch: 哎呀，多个人帮忙可是件大好事…那你等会儿，我、我得组织下语言，把昨晚的经历告诉你……
t_en: It's great that there are more people here to solve this case... H—Hold on, I'll explain what happened last night. Just let me gather my thoughts first...

t_ch: 好、好了。是这样…我、我昨天本来在守夜，结果正好撞见了那个「零件窃贼」！然后、然后…我、我就……
t_en: O—Okay. So... Last night, I was on patrol when I bumped into the thief stealing the parts! Then... then... I—I just...

t_ch: 你在害怕什么？
t_en: Why do you sound so terrified?

t_ch: 呜…我都快被吓死了，太可怕了！
t_en: Goodness, I was so scared! It was dreadful!

t_ch: 别激动，慢慢说。
t_en: Relax, explain it slowly.

t_ch: 呜…好，慢慢说……
t_en: Goodness... Okay. I'll explain slowly...

t_ch: 呃，别一上来就卡壳啊…
t_en: Don't panic. We're only getting started...

t_ch: 我、你、我、呃…不好意思…只、只是昨晚的事情太吓人了…我、我继续！
t_en: I... you... I—I'm sorry... Last night was just so terrifying... I—I'll continue!

t_ch: 我昨天晚上值岗的时候又冷又饿，于是就想着回一趟营地，把之前吃剩的罐头热一下当宵夜。
t_en: I was cold and hungry while on patrol last night, so I decided to return to the dwelling and heat up some leftover canned food for a snack.

t_ch: 结果我刚掀开帐篷…那个黑影，那个贼吓人的黑影就窜到了我的眼前！
t_en: But when I entered the tent... the thief's silhouette scurried across my vision!

t_ch: 当时营地里黑漆漆的一片，我没看清那毛贼的正脸，但我确信，那绝对是个凶残的家伙……
t_en: I couldn't see the thief's face because the tent was so dark, but I could tell he wasn't a nice guy...

t_ch: 因为、因为那家伙转身逃跑的时候，他身上带的刀具直接划过了我的脸！我当时真的以为要小命不保了……
t_en: B—Because when he turned to flee, he swung a knife right across my face! In that moment, I really thought I was going to die...

t_ch: 贴在我脸上的那种冰凉的感觉…还有我闻到的那股铁锈味…那家伙拿的绝对是什么金属利器！只有这一点，我绝对不会记错！
t_en: I could feel the blade's chill on my cheeks, and smell its rusty scent... The weapon that fella had was definitely a metal one! That's the only thing I'm certain of!

t_ch: 而、而且，我在黑暗中瞧见他的轮廓…那人的脑壳竟然是方形的！对，没错，特别方！
t_en: Also, I saw the outline of him in the dark... That fella's head was absolutely square! Yes, you heard me, square!

t_ch: ——你敢信吗，怎么会有人是方脑壳？！实在是太可怕了……
t_en: Can you believe that? Someone with a square head! It was truly terrifying...

t_ch: 方脑壳？！哦，天哪！
t_en: A square head? Oh my gosh!

t_ch: 唔，虽然听着有些奇怪…但这说不定也是一条线索。克拉拉记下来了。
t_en: It sounds bizarre, but it could be a clue. I'll jot it down.

t_ch: 你、你别说了，我害怕…
t_en: Please, I can't carry on. I'm too terrified...

t_ch: 呜，克、克拉拉也很害怕！但是…要把重要线索记下来……
t_en: I—I'm also terrified! But I still have to jot down the important clues...

t_ch: 呃，姑且也算是条信息…？
t_en: Uh, I guess that could be a clue...

t_ch: 啊，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}说得对！原来如此，克拉拉记下来了……
t_en: Oh, you're right, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}! I see! I'll jot it down...

t_ch: 叔叔，您先冷静一下…这么说来，那个窃贼应该随身带着金属利器，而且有个…呃，方脑袋…对吗？
t_en: Sir, please take a deep breath... So, the thief was armed with a metal weapon and had a... um, square head... correct?

t_ch: …对，肯定是这样没错。
t_en: Yes, that's correct.

t_ch: 唔…克拉拉没法通过这些线索联想到什么。{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}有什么想法吗？
t_en: Hmm... I'm struggling to make sense of these clues. Do you have any thoughts, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}?

t_ch: 没什么头绪…还有其他细节吗？
t_en: Not really... Is there anything else you noticed?

t_ch: 呜…对不住，人家太害怕了嘛！猛地一个黑影窜出来，谁还会关注那些细节啊……
t_en: I'm sorry... I was too scared! When a shadow leaps out at you, who could possibly pay attention to such tiny details?

t_ch: 看来没有更多信息了…我们现在知道了犯人的外貌特征，而且很凶暴。可是要上哪找这样的人呢？
t_en: It seems that's everything... We now know one of the culprit's physical traits and that he is extremely violent. But how can we track him down?

t_ch: 瞧你这样…不会是共犯吧！
t_en: Why are you being so evasive? Are you an accomplice?

t_ch: 你、你不要凭空污人清白！我都吓成这样了，怎么可能是共犯？呜呜……
t_en: How can you just accuse me like that!? I was terrified, how could I be an accomplice!? *sob* ...

t_ch: 对、对不起！那个…{F#大姐姐她}{M#大哥哥他}应该是开玩笑的…请您不要放在心上！
t_en: I'm sorry! {F#She}{M#He} was just joking... Please don't take {F#her}{M#him} seriously!

t_ch: 结案了，犯人就是方脑壳悍匪！
t_en: We've cracked the case! The culprit has a square head!

t_ch: 喔…不愧是{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！可我们要上哪找具备这种特征的人呢？
t_en: Oh! You've figured it out. But how are we going to track down someone with such an unusual appearance?

t_ch: ——啊，对了，对了！那家伙逃跑的时候慌不择路，撞倒了一些东西…好、好像还留下了一些痕迹之类的线索。
t_en: Ah, yes, I remember something! While the culprit was hurrying to escape, he knocked something over... He may have left some clues or something.

t_ch: 这是很重要的线索…！{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，我们等下一起去查看一下现场吧？
t_en: This is a crucial clue! {F#Big sister}{M#Big brother}, Let's go investigate the crime scene together!

t_ch: 那家伙应该是往那个方向跑的——现场没人动过，痕迹应该都还在…那、那就拜托这位{F#好姐姐}{M#好哥哥}帮忙抓贼了。
t_en: I think he ran that way. No one has touched the crime scene, so any traces he left behind should still be there... P—Please catch the thief for us.

t_ch: 山体上有几道突兀的痕迹，似乎是被某种金属利器剐蹭后留下的。痕迹的末端指向了远方的某处。
t_en: Some abrupt scratches left on the mountain which seem to be done by a metal weapon. The end of the scratches points to a distant place.

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，你看…这里的痕迹不像是自然形成的，会不会是犯人留下的呢？
t_en: Take a look at these marks... These are very suspicious. Could they have been left by the culprit?

t_ch: 痕迹向着那个方向去了…我们去那附近找找看吧。
t_en: They point in that direction... Let's go take a look.

t_ch: 此处附近似乎很少有人到访，但地上却留下了一串奇怪的剐蹭痕迹，直朝前方蜿蜒而去。
t_en: Few people would come to this place, but you can see some scratches on the ground, snaking their way forward.

t_ch: 这串痕迹…好奇怪，看上去不像是人类留下来的……
t_en: And look at these! How unusual... They don't appear to have been left by a human...

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，痕迹到这里就中断了…我们只能在这附近找找其他线索了。
t_en: The tracks stop here... I guess we'll have to look around for other clues.

t_ch: 不过，这附近是堆放机械垃圾的地方啊。难道……
t_en: This is where the mechanical waste is disposed of though. Could it be...

t_ch: 小心，我们不要打草惊蛇。
t_en: Be careful. We don't want to alert the culprit.

t_ch: 好、好的！克拉拉就跟在{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}身后。
t_en: O—Okay! I'll follow you, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}.

t_ch: 没想到这悍匪喜欢垃圾堆…
t_en: I didn't expect our thief to like waste heaps...

t_ch: 唔，克拉拉觉得…犯人应该不是喜欢垃圾堆，而是把这里当成藏身处了…啊，也有可能他在这里藏了赃物？
t_en: Um, I don't think the culprit actually likes waste heaps. He probably uses them as a hiding spot... Oh, maybe he hid the stolen parts here too!

t_ch: 偷零件的犯人，出来！面对我！
t_en: Come out and face me now, thief!

t_ch: 欸？！声音太大啦…如果犯人就在这里的话，肯定会被吓跑的！
t_en: Hey! You're being too loud... If the culprit is nearby, he'll just run away!

t_ch: 克拉拉觉得，我们还是分头…等等，那是什么声音？
t_en: Let's split up... Wait, what's that noise?

t_ch: 是个…小机器人？它是在翻找什么东西吗？ 可…史瓦罗先生最近好像没有发布这样的命令啊……
t_en: It's a... tiny robot? Is it searching for something? But... I don't remember Mr. Svarog issuing an order like this recently...

t_ch: 啊，不过它好像确实拿着叔叔阿姨们丢失的零件——
t_en: It looks like it's carrying the vagrants' missing parts...

t_ch: 说起来，我们之前见到的那些痕迹，还有金属刮痕……
t_en: And those tracks and metal scratches we noticed earlier...

t_ch: 看来小偷并不是「人」。
t_en: The thief doesn't appear to be a human.

t_ch: 嗯，看来{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}和我想的一样……
t_en: Hmm, it appears we're thinking the same thing...

t_ch: 遥控机器人…是高智商犯罪！
t_en: Remotely controlling a robot to steal parts... What an ingenious criminal!

t_ch: 唔…虽然听起来很离奇，但也不能排除这个可能性。
t_en: Hmm... That would be quite strange, yet such a scenario can't be ruled out.

t_ch: 确实是方脑壳，但不像悍匪…
t_en: It does have a square head, but it doesn't look like a criminal...

t_ch: 确实…这个型号的机器人，应该没有配备金属利器才对。
t_en: Yes... This robot model probably doesn't have any kind of metal weapon.

t_ch: 仔细看来，这个小机器人…不像是贝洛伯格制式的机器人呢。简直像是…用废弃金属材料随意焊接出来的——咦，它好像还有一只小小的机械臂……？
t_en: This small robot... doesn't appear to be a Belobog creation. It looks like it's been welded together from random scrap metal materials... Huh, it appears to have a small mechanical arm...

t_ch: …啊，它发现我们了！
t_en: Ah, it's noticed us!
█I███human. ██injured? ██no████hurt! ██please don't██.

t_ch: …克拉拉，你懂机械语吗？
t_en: ...Clara, do you speak robot?

t_ch: 克、克拉拉也不懂…但它似乎并不打算逃跑，也不打算攻击我们……
t_en: I—I don't understand what it's saying... but it doesn't appear to be trying to flee or attack us...

t_ch: 少废话，把偷走的零件交出来！
t_en: Anyway, hand over the stolen parts, right now!

t_ch: 啊，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，请等一下！你看它，似乎没打算逃跑，也不打算攻击我们……
t_en: Ah, wait, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}! Look! It doesn't appear to be trying to flee or attack us...

t_ch: 别打我，我投降！好汉饶命啊！
t_en: Don't hit me. I surrender! Please have mercy!

t_ch: 大、{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，别害怕！你看它的样子，似乎并没有要伤害我们的意思……
t_en: Don't be scared! Look! It doesn't seem like it wants to hurt us...

t_ch: 不过，它好像想说些什么——我、人、请勿、伤害……
t_en: It appears to be trying to say something: I, human, please, don't hurt...

t_ch: …难道是在说：「人类，请不要伤害我」吗？
t_en: ...Is it trying to say: "Please don't hurt me, humans"?
█Human███! Parts██Rivet██need████repair██need██! Town.
█ Parts███ need██? █ █Need █ ███ need! ██ need██! Hurt██ don't.

t_ch: 它的表达…很混乱，但克拉拉大概能理解……
t_en: It appears to have a malfunction, but I think I can understand it...

t_ch: 它似乎十分需要这些零件，让我们不要伤害它……
t_en: It seems that it needs these parts, and it's begging us not to hurt it...

t_ch: 克拉拉…从来没见过这样异常的机器人。它…究竟是怎么回事……？
t_en: I—I've never seen a robot with this kind of malfunction before... What happened to it...?

t_ch: 不伤害可以，先还零件。
t_en: We won't hurt this robot, but it must return the parts first.

t_ch: 唔…虽然这个小机器人似乎有沟通的意愿，但说出来的内容却很混乱……
t_en: Although this small robot appears to want to communicate, its speech is all scrambled...

t_ch: 可能是逻辑语言中枢出了问题吧。这样的话，这个小家伙应该听不懂我们说的话……
t_en: Perhaps there's an issue with its language module. This little guy probably can't understand what we're saying either...

t_ch: 我的耐心是有极限的…
t_en: My patience is limited...

t_ch: 噢噢噢噢——我要上了！
t_en: Chaaaaarge! Prepare to fight!
█Run███escape! Open██fire██workshop████chase██don't██!

t_ch: 啊！它好像要逃走了……！
t_en: Ah! It's running away!

t_ch: 不、不能让它跑掉！它还拿着叔叔阿姨们的零件呢！
t_en: No! We can't let it escape! It still has the vagrants' parts!

t_ch: 怎、怎么办…只能想办法先制止它了！{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，拜托了，下手轻一点！克拉拉之后会想办法修好它的……
t_en: What should we do? We gotta stop it! But, please don't be too rough on it! I'll figure out a way to repair it later...

t_ch: █ 伤害██痛觉█ 不██伤害 █ █不要 █ █疼痛█痛苦█ 对不起 ██ 零件██对不起。
t_en: █ Hurt██pain█ no██hurt █ █don't █ █pain█suffer█ sorry ██ parts██sorry.

t_ch: █ 对不起██疼███痛█ 不█痛█对起 █ █对不起 █ ██疼痛█ 对不起对不起 ██ 对不起对██苦██不起██████对不起！
t_en: █ Sorry██hurt███pain█ no█pain█sorry █ █sorry █ ██pain█ sorry ██ sorry██suffer██sorry██████sorry!

t_ch: █ 对不█疼███苦███苦痛█疼痛█ 不█痛█对起 █ █对█████起 █ ██疼█████████痛█ 对不起对痛苦不起对不起痛对不起对疼不起 ████████████？！
t_en: █Sorry█hurt███suffer█pain█no█pain█sorry██so█████rry███pain█████████hurt█sorry suffer sorry sorry pain sorry ouch pain sorry████████████!

t_ch: …为什么？
t_en: Why?
Sorry██pain███suffer██sorry████████...

t_ch: 它好像…彻底没法行动了……
t_en: It seems to have stopped working...

t_ch: 我不懂，刚才那是什么情况？
t_en: I don't understand what just happened.

t_ch: 它在…求饶？大概是这样吧…克拉拉也不明白。
t_en: Was it begging for mercy? I'm not sure what happened either.

t_ch: 它是不是一直在说…
t_en: Was it saying...

t_ch: 是啊，可是为什么……？
t_en: Yes, but why?

t_ch: 克拉拉…感觉不太好……
t_en: I... feel bad about this...

t_ch: …总之，我们先把这个小家伙和零件带回去吧。克拉拉有把握…不对，克拉拉一定会修好它。
t_en: I guess we should return this little fella and the parts. I think I can... I'll repair it.

t_ch: 万一修不好的话，我就去请教史瓦罗先生——史瓦罗先生总会有办法的……
t_en: And if I can't, I'll ask Mr. Svarog for help. He always knows what to do...

t_ch: 开始评估个体状况——稍等，克拉拉。
t_en: Initiating assessment of the robot's condition. Please wait, Clara.

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生评估期间，我们先给这个小家伙做个简单的检查吧。
t_en: While Mr. Svarog is assessing this little fella, let's give it a quick checkup ourselves.

t_ch: 咦…？这个小家伙的涂装，还有它身上这些定制的部件，以及部件连接的手法…好奇怪。
t_en: Huh? This paint job and these custom parts. And the way the parts are attached... It's all quite strange.

t_ch: 不过，有一点克拉拉可以确定——这个小家伙没有受到遥控，它是完全凭着自身的意志在行动……
t_en: But one thing is for certain... This little fella is not under remote control. He's operating entirely of his own accord...

t_ch: 唔…它的结构实在是太异常了。克拉拉从没见过这种结构的机器人。
t_en: Its design is, well, unique. I've never seen a robot with a design like this before.

t_ch: 这简直就像是…什么人胡乱组装出来的。小家伙的发明者…可能根本没考虑过系统能不能正常运行——
t_en: It really seems like something that was thrown together rather quickly. Its creator most likely didn't consider whether the system would function properly or not...

t_ch: 难道说，它是被人遗弃的「机械宠物」失败品吗…？
t_en: Is it a failed robot pet creation that has been abandoned...?

t_ch: 可假如它是宠物的话…为什么要自己收集零件呢？
t_en: But if it's a pet... then why would it gather parts itself?

t_ch: 「机械宠物」是什么？
t_en: What is a "robot pet"?

t_ch: 我好像在哪里听说过。
t_en: I think I've heard the term "robot pet" before.

t_ch: 这地方对宠物的理解还挺自由…
t_en: This place has a somewhat lax definition of pets...

t_ch: 克拉拉听说，营地里有的叔叔阿姨会把偶尔捡到的小机器人当成宠物来养。
t_en: I've heard that some vagrants in the dwelling keep the little robots they find as pets.

t_ch: 有些流浪者还会动手改装自己的宠物，他们管那叫…「宠物升级」。
t_en: Some vagrants also make modifications to their pets, which they call... pet upgrades.

t_ch: 虽然会养「机械宠物」的人不算多，但时间一长，他们也形成了一个小圈子，交流饲养机器人的心得，甚至还会让它们…互相战斗。
t_en: Pets like that are pretty rare, but some of the folks who have them have formed a small community to share ideas. Some are interested in nurturing robots. Others... force them into battle.

t_ch: 如果克拉拉没记错的话…被这个小家伙偷走的零件，原本就是拿来给「机械宠物」升级的。
t_en: If my memory serves me right, the parts this little fella stole were originally designed for that kind of pet upgrade.

t_ch: 战斗？太没人性了！
t_en: Battle each other!? That's awful!

t_ch: 那些叔叔阿姨…不，应该说是下层区的大多数人，都只把机器人看作工具而已。
t_en: The vagrants... or, to be more accurate, the majority of people in the Underworld regard robots as nothing more than tools.

t_ch: 但克拉拉不这么认为…小机器人也有自己的想法，也会感受到疼痛……
t_en: But I disagree. Robots have their own thoughts and feelings...

t_ch: …{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}能理解克拉拉的想法…真是太好了。
t_en: It's great that you understand what I'm saying...

t_ch: 听起来还挺有意思的。
t_en: That sounds pretty interesting.

t_ch: …欸？原来{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}也这么觉得吗？
t_en: That's not the word I'd use.

t_ch: 嗯…也对，毕竟大多数人都只把机器人看作工具而已…但克拉拉还是会尊重{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，还有大家的选择。
t_en: I guess most people only see robots as tools. I'll try not to hold it against you.

t_ch: 奇怪的知识增加了…
t_en: I've learned some unusual things...

t_ch: 唉…克拉拉也觉得这种「机械宠物」很奇怪。
t_en: *sigh*... This robot pet phenomenon really gets to me.

t_ch: 那个…抱歉，克拉拉只是突然有些感触。我们还是继续研究怎么修复小机器人吧。
t_en: Erm... I'm sorry, I just suddenly felt some strong emotions. Let's get back to work on repairing the small robot.

t_ch: 唔，这个插口的位置…好奇怪。芯片的位置藏得非常隐秘，不仔细找的话根本发现不了……
t_en: This jack's placement is... odd. The chip is buried deep inside. If you don't look closely enough, you won't even find it...

t_ch: 评估个体状况完成。认定外壳损毁程度为——<unbreak>98.2%</unbreak>。
t_en: Assessment of the robot's condition is complete. The shell damage is estimated to be <unbreak>98.2%</unbreak>.

t_ch: 评估结果：无法彻底复原。建议——抛弃原有外部装甲，将核心模组及芯片移入新外壳，保留表层电路及相关传动装置。
t_en: The assessment concludes that a total recovery is impossible. It is recommended that the robot's external shell be removed and replaced while keeping the outer circuitry and related transmission devices.

t_ch: 啊，不愧是史瓦罗先生，是个好主意！也就是说，想修好它的话…我们就得去找一副新的机器人外壳，对吧？
t_en: You can always rely on Mr. Svarog to come up with a brilliant idea! So, I guess we'll have to go find a new robot shell to fix it, right?

t_ch: 那么在哪里才能买到呢？
t_en: Where can we buy one?

t_ch: 唔…克拉拉也不知道有没有人在卖这种东西。不过……
t_en: Hmm... I'm not sure if anyone sells this kind of thing, however...

t_ch: 那我去把其他机器人的扒下来！
t_en: I'll go and strip them from the other robots!

t_ch: 请、请不要用蛮力解决问题，其实还有其它办法……
t_en: Please don't use violent methods to solve this issue. There are other options...

t_ch: 想找机器人外壳的话，机械墓场附近应该会有现成的吧？虽说大家都叫它「墓场」…但那里其实藏着不少有用的部件呢。
t_en: There must be some robot shells lying around the machine graveyard. Everyone calls it a graveyard, but there are actually lots of useful parts buried there.

t_ch: 那个…克拉拉想先把机械零件还给叔叔阿姨们，之后再继续确认这个小家伙的状况。去机械墓场找外壳的事，能先麻烦{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}吗？
t_en: I'm gonna return the parts to the vagrants first, then I'll check on the little fella. Can I trust you to find a shell in the machine graveyard, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 捡破烂是我的特长…
t_en: I'm an expert at scavenging...

t_ch: …真的吗？{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}说不定有做淘金者的资质呢。
t_en: Really? You could have what it takes to be a prospector, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}.

t_ch: 实在不行就去扒一副外壳下来…
t_en: I can always go grab one from another robot if needed...

t_ch: 请、请不要再说这种话了，克拉拉也是会生气的……
t_en: Please, please don't say things like that. You'll upset me...

t_ch: 那…这件事就麻烦{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}了。
t_en: Then I'll entrust this task to you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}可以去门口找托比——虽然看上去有些简陋，但它其实是最熟悉机械墓场的机器人了。就让托比给{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}带路吧。
t_en: Look for Toby at the front entrance. His appearance is... crude, but he's the robot most familiar with the machine graveyard. Just follow Toby.

t_ch: 您好，我是托比，很高兴为您服务。
t_en: Hello, my name is Toby. It is my honor to serve you.

t_ch: 您好，克拉拉小姐的客人，不好也行，您请便。很高兴为您服务。
t_en: Hello, guest of Miss Clara. Please do as you wish! It's my honor to serve you.

t_ch: 托比，你能为我带路吗？
t_en: Toby, can you show me the way?

t_ch: 克拉拉说你有些简陋…
t_en: Clara described you as primitive...

t_ch: 为您播放趣味小知识一则：托比其实并不简陋。
t_en: Allow me to share a fun fact: Toby is not a primitive robot.

t_ch: 已收到克拉拉小姐的指令，为您规划在「机械墓场」的游览路线。本机将持续为您导航航航航——
t_en: Miss Clara has assigned me the task of planning your sightseeing route in the machine graveyard. I will guide you down the path.

t_ch: 您是否需要开启「超级加速」模式？若是，请原地逆时针旋转十周后再顺时针旋转<unbreak>270</unbreak>°以确认。
t_en: Would you like to engage in Super Speed mode? If so, please confirm by spinning counterclockwise <unbreak>10</unbreak> times, then clockwise by <unbreak>270</unbreak> degrees.

t_ch: 已到达第二个目标地点。该机体型号为「甲虫」，与本机为同一型号。
t_en: We have arrived at our second destination. This module is called the Beetle, which is the same as mine.

t_ch: 该机体报废原因为——指令解析错误后狂奔<unbreak>7.1</unbreak>公里，导致电机结构起火，引发核心模组熔毁。
t_en: This machine was scrapped because it accelerated erratically for <unbreak>7.1</unbreak> kilometers after it incorrectly interpreted a command, causing the engine to catch fire and the core module to melt down.

t_ch: （居然还有这么离奇的报废原因……）
t_en: (That is such an odd reason to be scrapped...)

t_ch: （但外壳看起来状况不错，姑且带上吧。）
t_en: (However, the shell appears to be in good condition, so I'll bring it with me.)

t_ch: 即将前往第二个目标地点，请坐好、扶稳，系紧安全带。
t_en: We are on our way to the second destination. Please take a seat, buckle up, and hold tight.

t_ch: 注意：您已超速。请严格遵守贝洛伯格交通法规——道路没几条，安全第一条。
t_en: CAUTION: You have exceeded the speed limit. Please obey Belobog traffic laws. On the road, safety is the top priority.

t_ch: 已抵达第一个目标地点。该机体型号为「齿狼」，报废原因为——在「机械宠物」决斗中受到贯穿伤，引发电锯燃料箱殉爆。
t_en: We have arrived at our first destination. This model is known as the Direwolf, and was scrapped after it sustained piercing damage during a battle with another robot pet, which caused the chainsaw's fuel tank to burst.

t_ch: （这「机械宠物」决斗听起来有点恐怖……）
t_en: (Robot pet battles sound a little frightening...)

t_ch: （装甲内部因高热熔合得乱七八糟。看来没办法带走，可惜了。）
t_en: (The inside of the armor is a total mess due to being melted by the heat. Unfortunately, it appears to be useless.)

t_ch: 本次导航已完成。请注意侧后方来车、敌人及自动机兵。
t_en: Your trip has concluded. Please keep an eye out for vehicles approaching from the back or sides, as well as enemies and automatons.

t_ch: 导航已经完成，感谢您对托比的信任。
t_en: Your trip has concluded. I appreciate you putting your trust in Toby.

t_ch: 已自动为您生成五星好评，不用谢！
t_en: A five-star review for this trip has been automatically generated for you. You're welcome!

t_ch: 这是托比应该做的！
t_en: It was Toby's honor!

t_ch: 克拉拉，到休息时间了。我反对在负面情绪影响下维修机械，这将对你的身体和精神状况造成损伤。
t_en: Clara, it's time for a break. You shouldn't repair machines when you have negative emotions. It's bad for your physical and mental health.

t_ch: …我没事的，史瓦罗先生。请您放心吧。
t_en: Don't worry, Mr. Svarog. I'm fine.

t_ch: …啊，{NICKNAME}{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，你回来啦？怎么样，托比带你找到机械外壳了吗？
t_en: Oh, {NICKNAME}, you're back already? How did it go? Did Toby help you find a shell?

t_ch: （把新外壳交给克拉拉）
t_en: (Hand the new shell to Clara)

t_ch: 这副机械外壳…唔…看样子，似乎不是小家伙原来的款式呢？
t_en: This shell... Hmm... It doesn't appear to be the same design as this little fella's...

t_ch: 不过问题应该不大，这些小型机器人的结构其实都相差不多——辛苦你跑这一趟啦，{NICKNAME}{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}。
t_en: But it shouldn't matter. The structures of these small robots are nearly identical. Thank you for bringing this to me, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 克拉拉已经把小机器人的芯片拆卸完了，现在…嘿咻…只要把这一大堆线路结构移接到新的外壳里就好……
t_en: I've finished extracting that chip from the small robot. Now all that's left to do is move all of this circuitry into the new shell...

t_ch: 唔…虽然外形变化很大…不过总的来说，应该还算成功吧？
t_en: Although its appearance has changed a lot, I think we did a good job.

t_ch: 这么说来，克拉拉刚才做的事情，和那些叔叔阿姨们说的「宠物升级」是不是有些类似呢……
t_en: Hmm, but what I just did... Is it any different from those pet upgrades that the vagrants perform...?
███████not██compatible██in-██compatible██!?

t_ch: 欸？它发出的声音，好像有点奇怪？
t_en: Huh? It's making a strange sound.
█████Incompatible████in-████!

t_ch: 糟、糟糕了！不会是出了什么故障吧——
t_en: Wh—What's going on? Is it malfunctioning?

t_ch: ██异常██异█常██异常异█异常异常异常异常██████——
t_en: ██Incompatible██in█com██incomatiblein█incompatibleincompatibleincompatibleincompatible██████—

t_ch: ——风险等级评估：高。克拉拉，立刻远离危险来源！
t_en: Risk level: High. Clara, get away, now!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}，小心，它失去控制了！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, watch out! It's out of control!

t_ch: ██异常██异█常██异常异█异常异常异常异常██████——
t_en: ██Incompatible██in█com██incomatiblein█incompatibleincompatibleincompatibleincompatible██████—
█████Termi█nate███terminate██████.

t_ch: 呼…呼…刚才真是…好险呀。幸好史瓦罗先生和{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}反应快……
t_en: Phew... that was close. Fortunately, Mr. Svarog and {NICKNAME} acted fast...

t_ch: 到底发生了什么，为什么小家伙会…是兼容性出了问题吗？
t_en: Why did the little fella... Was there a compatibility issue?

t_ch: 正在运行故障诊断……
t_en: Troubleshooting...

t_ch: ——评估结果：芯片搭载系统与新硬件不兼容导致的运行故障。
t_en: Assessment: The system on the chip and the new hardware are incompatible, causing the robot to behave erratically.

t_ch: 当前故障已排除。风险等级评估：低。允许解除警戒。
t_en: The current issue has been resolved. Risk level: Low. Alert lifted.

t_ch: 经过刚才的战斗，小家伙好像已经暂时适应这副新外壳了？太好了……
t_en: It appears to have adjusted to its new shell after the battle. That's great...

t_ch: 不过…克拉拉还想再进一步观察一下它的适应状态。做兼容性相关的测试应该需要几天…希望到时候它可以恢复正常。
t_en: But... I'd like to keep a closer eye on it for a while. Compatibility tests should take a few days. Hopefully, it will be all back to normal by then.

t_ch: 另外…我很好奇这个小家伙的来历。{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}，如果可以的话…能请你之后再来这里一趟吗？
t_en: I'm also curious about where this little fella came from. Could you please come back later, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 克拉拉想把事情调查清楚…这可能需要{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}的帮助。
t_en: I need to look into something... I may require your assistance.

t_ch: 听你的。
t_en: Okay.

t_ch: 那明天见，克拉拉。
t_en: See you tomorrow, Clara.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}！等把这个小家伙修好了，克拉拉会用短信联系你的。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}! I'll text you when this little fella is fixed.

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生，我想给这个小家伙起个名字…你觉得什么样的名字好听呢？
t_en: Mr. Svarog, I'd like to give this little fella a name. Any ideas for a good name?

t_ch: ——调用资源：姓名库。检索开始——艾比，艾比盖尔，艾妲，艾迪森……
t_en: Accessing name database. Search results: Abby, Abigail, Ada, Addison...

t_ch: （之后再来找克拉拉吧。）
t_en: (I should come back to Clara later.)

t_ch: （看来它还没恢复正常…之后再来看看吧。）
t_en: (Looks like it hasn't gone back to normal yet... I should come back later.)

t_ch: 克拉拉将机器人的核心移入了新的机械外壳中……
t_en: Clara transferred the robot's Core Module into the new Robot Shell...

t_ch: 克拉拉将帕斯卡的核心移入了新的机械外壳中……
t_en: Clara transferred Pascal's Core Module into the new Robot Shell...

t_ch: 克拉拉将帕斯卡的核心移植到了报废的自动机兵「灰熊」体内……
t_en: Clara transferred Pascal's Core Module into the scrapped Automaton Grizzly...

t_ch: （帕斯卡应该还在断电维护中…过段时间再来看看吧。）
t_en: (Pascal should be under offline maintenance now... Come check him after some time.)
███You██Pascal█very██ █like████Clara██!

t_ch: 嘿嘿～帕斯卡，好乖好乖。
t_en: Hehe, Good boy. Pascal.

t_ch: 基于当前采集到的数据，得出结论：建议将机械单元帕斯卡格式化后纳入基地网络，以免威胁聚落居民的财产及人身安全。
t_en: Based on the information gathered, it is recommended that Pascal's data be formatted and integrated into the base network so that he doesn't threaten the property or safety of the camp's residents.

t_ch: 嗯…克拉拉明白，这样做可能是最合理的选择。但帕斯卡它……
t_en: I know this is the most sensible option, but Pascal...

t_ch: 维修工作如何了？
t_en: How are the repairs going?

t_ch: 欢迎，外来者。在克拉拉的努力下，维修工作已经顺利完成。
t_en: Welcome, outsider. Thanks to Clara's hard work, the repairs were completed successfully.

t_ch: 是的，小家伙现在已经活蹦乱跳了。只是…还有些部分没有完全恢复正常。
t_en: Yes, the little fella is alive and well again. It's just that... some parts aren't completely back to normal yet.

t_ch: 帕斯卡？这是它的名字吗？
t_en: Pascal? Is that his name?

t_ch: 是的！为了给它起个合适的名字，克拉拉和史瓦罗先生一起找了很久很久……
t_en: Yes! Mr. Svarog and I spent a long time coming up with a suitable name...

t_ch: 哪些部分不正常？
t_en: What about Pascal isn't normal, exactly?

t_ch: 唔…让克拉拉想想该怎么解释……
t_en: Well... How should I put it...

t_ch: 放弃吧…
t_en: I think you should give up...

t_ch: 唔…那可不行！克拉拉不能接受……
t_en: Absolutely not! I'm not going to give up...
███You██Pascal█good███I██am called██name██!

t_ch: 啊，看！{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，帕斯卡在和你打招呼呢。只是…它说的话还是有些混乱。
t_en: Oh look, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}! Pascal is greeting you. It's just... his speech is still a little confusing.

t_ch: 从先前的检查中得出的结果——该机体核心模组中储存的校验信息与现有硬件不兼容。
t_en: The examination conducted earlier revealed that the information stored in this robot's core module is incompatible with the new hardware.

t_ch: 此外，该机体核心模组中亦见大量逻辑坏道。在对其进行深入扫描时识别到大量信息噪音，内容不详。
t_en: Furthermore, many defective networks were detected in the core module. A significant amount of muddled information with unknown content was discovered during a deep scan.

t_ch: 简单地说，帕斯卡的脑袋里塞满了来源不明的「垃圾」。这些「垃圾」，再加上跟核心不兼容的硬件，让它忘记该怎么说话了。
t_en: Simply put, Pascal's brain is filled with junk information of unknown origin. This junk, along with hardware that's incompatible with his core, caused him to lose his ability to communicate.

t_ch: 还好，除了语言模块之外，帕斯卡已经基本没有问题……
t_en: Fortunately, apart from the language module, Pascal is still functioning properly...

t_ch: 但该个体仍然存在故障可能，概率约为<unbreak>44.7%</unbreak>。重复建议：将机械单元帕斯卡格式化后纳入基地网络统一管理。
t_en: However, the robot still has a <unbreak>44.7%</unbreak> chance of malfunctioning. It's recommended that Pascal's data be formatted and integrated into the base network.

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生，我明白…只是，克拉拉觉得帕斯卡很特别。它身上有些其他小机器人不具备的东西……
t_en: Mr. Svarog, I understand, but I think Pascal is different. He possesses a quality that other small robots don't have...

t_ch: 某种程度上，我感觉它和史瓦罗先生有些…相似。克拉拉没法忽视这种独特之处……
t_en: In some ways, I believe he is similar to Mr. Svarog. I can't overlook this unique quality...

t_ch: 可以再给我些时间吗，史瓦罗先生？克拉拉想和{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}再继续调查一段时间…如果实在没有进展，再把帕斯卡纳入网络也不晚。
t_en: Mr. Svarog, could you give me a little more time? I want to investigate this further. If we don't find anything, you can integrate Pascal into the network.

t_ch: 理解。我尊重你的意愿，克拉拉。在此期间，我会确保该个体不对他人人身及财产安全造成影响。
t_en: Understood, Clara. I respect your wish. In the meantime, I'll make sure that this robot doesn't endanger other people or property.

t_ch: 那么…我们现在该怎么办？
t_en: So... What should we do now?

t_ch: 唔，之前和史瓦罗先生一起讨论的对策，克拉拉想稍微总结一下。
t_en: I'd like to recap what I discussed with Mr. Svarog earlier.

t_ch: 直接格式化算了，一了百了…
t_en: We should just format the robot and call it a day...

t_ch: █淬火██勿██不要██归█回██伤害█！
t_en: █Tempering██no██don't██return█back██hurt█!

t_ch: 对不起，克拉拉自作主张要{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}帮忙一起调查……
t_en: I know this is asking a lot...

t_ch: 但是…这件事对克拉拉来说真的很重要！拜托{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，请你再帮克拉拉一次吧。
t_en: But... it's very important to me! Please... help me one more time.

t_ch: 好吧，真是拿你没办法。
t_en: Argh, if I have to...

t_ch: 唔，谢谢…又要给{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}添麻烦了…！
t_en: Oh, th—thanks then... I'm so sorry to trouble you again, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 开个玩笑而已，我们走吧。
t_en: I was just kidding. Let's go!

t_ch: 咻…是玩笑就好。
t_en: Phew... As long as you're not really annoyed with me.

t_ch: 其实，我们要做的事情和之前一样——找到新的外壳，把帕斯卡的核心安装进去，看看硬件是否兼容。
t_en: Let's begin by doing exactly what we did before. Find a new shell, put Pascal's core in it, and see if the hardware is compatible.

t_ch: 分析结果显示，机械单元帕斯卡的原生硬件为「齿狼」或「灰熊」型。若能将该个体核心接入对应框架，其语言功能恢复正常的概率为<unbreak>87.3%</unbreak>。
t_en: According to the analysis, Pascal's original hardware was a Direwolf or Grizzly model. If we can put its core into the appropriate shell, there's an <unbreak>87.3%</unbreak> chance that its language module will return to normal.

t_ch: 机械墓场里有各种各样的废弃机器人…我们先去那边找找看吧。
t_en: There are all kinds of abandoned robots in the machine graveyard. Let's go take a look.

t_ch: 这里…还是老样子呢。到处都是失去自主功能的机器人……
t_en: It's the same old place. Everywhere you look, there are robots that have lost the ability to function...

t_ch: 啊…{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}，看那边——
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}, take a look over there!

t_ch: 这就是「齿狼」型的自动机兵了，不过…看上去也被进行了某种改造，大概又是个被遗弃的机械宠物……
t_en: This is an Automaton Direwolf, but it appears to have been modified. It's most likely another abandoned robot pet...

t_ch: 这台机器看上去已经失去机能了，但{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}接近它的时候还是要谨慎一些……
t_en: Although it doesn't seem to be functioning, you should still be cautious when approaching it...

t_ch: 滋…滋……
t_en: Dzz... Dzz...

t_ch: 发现…入侵…
t_en: Intruder... Detected...

t_ch: 净化…净化！
t_en: Eliminate... Eliminate!

t_ch: 糟了，它还醒着——{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，小心！
t_en: Oh no, it's still active. Watch out!

t_ch: 入侵者…擅自…打破……
t_en: Intruders... Violate... Protocol...

t_ch: 终结…！协议…！彻底清除！
t_en: Terminate...! Protocol...! Eliminate!

t_ch: 这个大家伙已经彻底失控了…那就先让它安静下来吧，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}。
t_en: This big fella is completely out of control... Let's quiet him down!

t_ch: 嘿咻、嘿咻……
t_en: Argh...

t_ch: 啊，找到了！它的核心在这里。我要把它装起来，带回去……
t_en: Here it is! This is the core. I'll pack it up and take it with us...

t_ch: 唔，接下来只要把它的外壳拖回去就好。
t_en: Hmm, then all that's left is to drag the shell back.

t_ch: 放着我来，你先休息吧。
t_en: Leave it to me. You should take a break.

t_ch: …没关系，克拉拉也很有力气！我要再把核心塞紧一点，免得掉出来……
t_en: It's okay, I'm strong too! I'll tuck the core in a little tighter so it doesn't fall out...

t_ch: 这家伙的核心你也要回收吗？
t_en: You're taking its core too?

t_ch: 根据刚才的状况来看，它的核心应该还没完全报废，有修好的可能性。
t_en: Judging from how active the robot was, its core isn't just scrap metal. It should be repairable.

t_ch: 每个机器人…只要还有修好的可能，克拉拉就不能放着不管。所以…我想再努把力，试着把它们一并修好。
t_en: I can't let any robot go to waste if it can be repaired. So... I'm going to try to fix them all.

t_ch: 我理解你。
t_en: Okay, I understand.

t_ch: 谢谢{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}…能够理解克拉拉的想法。
t_en: Thank you... for understanding me.

t_ch: 那要先修帕斯卡，还是先修它？
t_en: So, should we fix Pascal or this one first?

t_ch: 嗯…还是先修帕斯卡吧，毕竟我们先捡到了那个小家伙嘛。而且，史瓦罗先生也说了，如果放着不管，它还有可能会做出不好的事……
t_en: Let's start with Pascal. We found him first, after all. Also, like Mr. Svarog warned, if he's not supervised, he might cause trouble...

t_ch: 其实，克拉拉有个好大好大的保险箱——里面全都是我在下层收集到的、还有修复可能的核心模组。
t_en: You know, I have a large safe full of core modules that I've gathered around the Underworld.

t_ch: 我每天都在想办法修好它们，让小机器人们恢复正常的生活。只是…只有克拉拉和史瓦罗先生两个人，修复的效率还远远不够。
t_en: Every day, I try to figure out how to repair some of them and return the robots to normal. Except... there are a lot more than Mr. Svarog and I can handle on our own.

t_ch: 但是克拉拉相信，大家总有一天都会被修好…所以，还不能松懈，必须加倍努力才行！
t_en: But I'm sure I can fix them all one day... We can't relax yet. We must work harder!

t_ch: 真是个好孩子啊，克拉拉。
t_en: You're a good kid, Clara.

t_ch: 能被{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}夸奖，克拉拉很开心……
t_en: Thank you, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}. It makes me happy to hear that.

t_ch: 美好的愿望通常很难实现。
t_en: That's a great dream, but a difficult one to realize.

t_ch: 我明白。史瓦罗先生和{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}一直都在帮助我…克拉拉一定会努力的。
t_en: I know. You and Mr. Svarog have helped me so much, someone needs to do the same for those poor robots... I've got to do my best.

t_ch: 机械专家希露瓦可以来帮忙！
t_en: I can get Serval to help us! She's an expert mechanic.

t_ch: 真的吗？克拉拉好期待…！
t_en: Really? I can't wait!

t_ch: 啊，光顾着聊天，都快忘记时间了。
t_en: Ah, I was talking so much that I totally lost track of time.

t_ch: 我们快回去吧，不然史瓦罗先生和帕斯卡要等着急啦。
t_en: Let's head back, otherwise Mr. Svarog and Pascal will get worried.

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生，我们回来啦。
t_en: Mr. Svarog, we're back.

t_ch: ██欢迎█归█！回█{NICKNAME}█。█克拉拉█铆█！█败██？失██——
t_en: ██Welcome█back█! return█{NICKNAME}█. █Clara█Rivet█!█Fail██-ure██?

t_ch: 欢迎回来，克拉拉、{NICKNAME}。我对该个体的进一步解析工作已经完成。
t_en: Welcome back, Clara. I've completed my further analysis of this robot.

t_ch: 发现什么了吗？
t_en: What have you discovered?

t_ch: 帕斯卡没去搞破坏吧？
t_en: Pascal didn't cause any trouble, did he?

t_ch: 解析期间未见任何异常行为，但个体核心模组中信息噪音的比重仍在快速上升——预计在三小时后，其异常概率将会上升至<unbreak>76.1%</unbreak>。
t_en: Although no abnormal behavior was detected during the analysis, the muddled information in the robot's core module is still rapidly increasing. The risk of abnormal behavior is expected to rise to <unbreak>76.1%</unbreak> in three hours.
██Lost█Pascal█. █Protect█good██! █Hurt██? No██...

t_ch: …看来，留给我们的时间不多了。还有其他细节吗，史瓦罗先生？
t_en: It appears we don't have much time left. Mr. Svarog, have you discovered anything else?

t_ch: 先别着急说，让我歇会儿…
t_en: Wait a minute, let me take a quick break...

t_ch: 那…请先告诉克拉拉吧，史瓦罗先生。
t_en: Okay... Then you can tell me first, Mr. Svarog.

t_ch: 简易结论：无法判断该个体的生产时间、用途及归属。
t_en: Conclusion: It is impossible to determine the time of manufacture, purpose, and ownership of this robot.

t_ch: 我在解析过程中挖掘出了该个体的序列号，并在下层区的自动机兵数据库中依次检索——
t_en: During my analysis, I extracted the robot's serial number and searched for it in the Underworld's automaton database...

t_ch: 查询得：与该个体拥有相同序列号的自动机兵多达<unbreak>32</unbreak>台。即使排除其中已报废的机体，剩余结果中仍有3台与关键词描述符合。
t_en: According to my search, there are <unbreak>32</unbreak> automatons with the same serial number as this one. Even after eliminating the scrapped ones, three automatons still match the keyword description.
█Failure█3██. █Back█Rivet1██!█fail██-ure██?

t_ch: 还有办法继续排除错误选项吗？
t_en: Is it possible to narrow down the search results further?

t_ch: 评估中…评估完成。结论：无法进一步排除。
t_en: Assessing... Assessment complete. Conclusion: Search results cannot be narrowed down any further.

t_ch: 三台符合关键词描述的机体分别位于熔锤镇、铆钉镇及锻炉镇——以上三座矿镇均已废弃，目前没有任何自动机兵在矿镇中形成聚落。
t_en: The three automatons that match the keyword descriptions are located in Moltammerville, Rivet Town, and Forge Town. All three of these mining towns have been abandoned, and no automatons have been discovered in these three sites recently.

t_ch: …看来线索又断了。
t_en: It appears that the trail has gone cold yet again.

t_ch: 它是不是想说什么？
t_en: Is Pascal trying to say something?

t_ch: 好像是呢…但克拉拉听不明白。
t_en: It appears so. I'm not sure what he's trying to say though.

t_ch: 谈话期间，该个体的故障率仍在不断上升——允许进入警戒状态。
t_en: While we were talking, the robot's failure rate increased. We should activate Alert mode.

t_ch: …这背后究竟藏着什么阴谋？！
t_en: Who's behind this plot!?

t_ch: 克拉拉也不知道…但如果能把目标锁定到三台机器身上的话，那……
t_en: I'm not sure, but if we can narrow it down to the three automatons, then...

t_ch: 或许我们还有机会能查清楚……
t_en: Maybe there's still a chance...
█Tempering█Tempering██. back█back█back to Rivet██!█fire██fire██!

t_ch: 帕斯卡的状态越来越不稳定了…我们还是先给它换上新外壳吧。
t_en: Pascal is becoming increasingly unstable... We should change his shell.

t_ch: 帕斯卡，接下来要给你换新外壳了哦。不会疼的，放心……
t_en: Pascal, I'm going to change your shell. Don't worry, it won't hurt...
█Back█back██. Back█back█back to Rivet██!█Rivet██Rivet██!

t_ch: █回来█等待██。继续█修█等待██失？败█等██！待██——
t_en: █Return█waiting██. continue█repair█waiting██failure█wait██—ing██!

t_ch: 还是不行吗…咦？帕斯卡，你怎么……
t_en: Still not functioning properly? What's wrong, Pascal?

t_ch: 危险，克拉拉——
t_en: Watch out, Clara...

t_ch: █失败█失失败██失败█失败败败█失败██败失失败██——
t_en: █Failure█failure██failure█failurelure█failure██failure██!

t_ch: ——帕斯卡？！啊…它、它要跑了！
t_en: Pascal!? No... He's running away!

t_ch: ——克拉拉！
t_en: Clara!

t_ch: 帕斯卡…它逃跑的方向，好像是铆钉镇那边？{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，我们快跟上！
t_en: Pascal... Did he run off towards Rivet Town? Let's follow him!

t_ch: 不，克拉拉，你不必出手。让我来处理这件事即可——我会派遣机器人追捕该个体……
t_en: No, Clara, just leave it to me. I'll order the robots to pursue this one...

t_ch: …不要，史瓦罗先生！求求你，请不要这么做……
t_en: No, Mr. Svarog! Please, don't...

t_ch: 帕斯卡的事情就交给克拉拉处理吧。毕竟，当初是我坚持要把它留下…所以，我必须负责到底。
t_en: Please leave Pascal to me. It was my decision to keep him in the first place, so I must take full responsibility.

t_ch: 我相信帕斯卡不会做出伤害人的事情…但，万一——万一真的出了什么意外——
t_en: I'm certain Pascal won't do anything to harm anyone, but if something goes wrong...

t_ch: …请让克拉拉亲手解决这件事。可以吗，史瓦罗先生？
t_en: Please let me handle this myself. Please, Mr. Svarog!

t_ch: 你忍心亲自抹除它的数据吗？
t_en: Can you endure the thought of erasing his data yourself?

t_ch: 我…克拉拉不能逃避责任。我一定会做到的。
t_en: I... can't shirk my responsibilities. I'll do it.

t_ch: 让克拉拉来解决吧。
t_en: Let Clara handle it.

t_ch: 没错…这件事因克拉拉而起，也只能由克拉拉来终结。
t_en: That's right... This all started with me and can only end with me.

t_ch: 我相信帕斯卡…它不会故意伤害别人的。
t_en: I'm certain Pascal would never do anything to harm anyone.

t_ch: 克拉拉，告诉我：你做出这个选择，是出于什么理由？
t_en: Clara, please tell me how you came to this conclusion.

t_ch: 帕斯卡身上有着其他小机器人没有的特质。那是…和我们——人类——拥有的「情感」很类似的东西。
t_en: Pascal possesses a quality that other small robots lack. It's kind of similar to the emotions we humans have.

t_ch: 同样的特质，克拉拉只在史瓦罗先生身上见到过……
t_en: I've only ever seen this quality in Mr. Svarog...

t_ch: 克拉拉觉得…这种「情感」，很温暖。克拉拉会不自觉地被它吸引，并试着用自己的方式给予反馈……
t_en: I feel... a warmth from these emotions. I feel drawn to this quality, and try to respond back in my own way...

t_ch: 但克拉拉也明白，这世界上的「情感」不全都是温暖的…如果有太多糟糕的「情感」跑出来搞破坏，就需要有人站出来阻止它们。
t_en: But I'm also aware that not all emotions in this world are positive... If there are too many negative emotions that go unchecked, then someone needs to step in and stop them.

t_ch: 能阻止那些糟糕「情感」的东西…就是「理性」和「责任」——这两样东西，也是克拉拉从史瓦罗先生身上获得的宝物。
t_en: The only things that can stop such negative emotions are rationality and responsibility. This is a valuable lesson I've learned from Mr. Svarog.

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生，我想帮帕斯卡打败那些糟糕的「情感」——我想主动承担起这份「责任」。
t_en: Mr. Svarog, I want to help Pascal overcome those negative emotions. I want to take on this responsibility.

t_ch: 这样…克拉拉就不用一直躲在史瓦罗先生的身后了。
t_en: That way... I won't have to hide behind Mr. Svarog anymore.

t_ch: …我明白了，克拉拉。我支持你的选择。
t_en: ...I understand, Clara. I support your decision.

t_ch: 除非事态发展到无可挽回的地步，否则我不会出手。
t_en: I won't intervene unless the situation risks spinning out of control.

t_ch: …谢谢你，史瓦罗先生。
t_en: Thank you, Mr. Svarog.

t_ch: 那…{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，我们出发吧！
t_en: So... Let's go, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}!

t_ch: …事情就是这样，史瓦罗先生。只是有一点，克拉拉还是不明白——帕斯卡它…为什么会被识别成裂界生物呢？
t_en: So that's what happened, Mr. Svarog. But there's one thing I don't understand... Why is Pascal being treated as a Fragmentum creature?

t_ch: 该个体长期使用遭受裂界污染的元件修复自身，因此会被连接基地网络的自动机兵识别为裂界生物。替换元件即可解决这一问题，不必担心。
t_en: This robot has been repairing itself using Fragmentum-contaminated components for an extended period of time. Therefore, automatons connected to the base network recognize it as a Fragmentum creature. This is a minor issue that can be resolved by replacing components, do not worry.

t_ch: 原来如此…克拉拉还想知道，在史瓦罗先生的机械网络中，有发生过类似的事件吗？
t_en: I see... Has Mr. Svarog ever witnessed a similar incident anywhere in the network?

t_ch: 无。接入网络的自动机兵会定时清理核心模组中的信息噪音。
t_en: No. Network-connected automatons regularly clear out muddled information in their core modules.

t_ch: 个体产生超脱控制的自我意识，其概率约为<unbreak>0.12%</unbreak>。对于未接入网络者而言，该概率提升为<unbreak>3.23%</unbreak>。
t_en: The likelihood of a robot gaining sentience beyond control is approximately <unbreak>0.12%</unbreak>. For those that aren't connected to the network, the chance climbs to <unbreak>3.23%</unbreak>.

t_ch: 提醒：该概率仅为理论估测。在下层区过去<unbreak>700</unbreak>年中，从未发现实际案例。
t_en: Please keep in mind that these probabilities are only estimates. Over the past <unbreak>700</unbreak> years, this has never happened in the Underworld.

t_ch: 所以…你也不知道该怎么处理？
t_en: So, you don't know what to do either?

t_ch: 不。与大多数人类的认知相反，容易被忽略的小概率事件，往往最有可能引致灾害。
t_en: Actually, I do. Contrary to popular belief, improbable events that are easily disregarded frequently result in catastrophes.

t_ch: 考虑到自动机兵仍有可能通过自身算力开发出情感模块，我对此类事件已有预案。
t_en: Given that automatons can still develop emotion modules using their own processing power, I have a backup plan in place.

t_ch: 但感觉这里的机器人都很聪明。
t_en: But the robots here appear to be quite intelligent...

t_ch: 为了降低用户学习成本，贝洛伯格所有自动机兵在设计之初都装载了语言模拟功能。它们会通过读取预设参数，模拟人类自然交流情景，生成合适的应答选项。
t_en: To make life easier for users, all of Belobog's automatons contain language simulation abilities. They generate appropriate responses by reading preset parameters and mimicking natural human conversations.

t_ch: 但这仅仅是一种模拟，不代表自动机兵拥有真正的情感智能。
t_en: This, however, is only a simulation, and it does not mean that the automatons have actual emotional intelligence.

t_ch: 但…史瓦罗先生是不一样的。
t_en: But, Mr. Svarog is different.

t_ch: 考虑到自动机兵仍有可能通过自身算力开发出情感模块，我对此已有预案。
t_en: Given that automatons can still develop emotion modules using their own processing power, I have a backup plan in place.

t_ch: 这么低的概率都撞上了？！
t_en: I can't believe such an improbable event has actually happened!

t_ch: 容易被忽略的小概率事件，往往最有可能引致灾害。
t_en: Improbable events that are easily disregarded frequently result in catastrophes.

t_ch: 考虑到自动机兵仍有可能通过自身算力开发出情感模块，我对此类事件已有预案。
t_en: Given that automatons can still develop emotion modules using their own processing power, I have a backup plan in place.

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生总是能考虑到所有情况，合理地解决各种问题…克拉拉暂时还做不到这点。
t_en: Mr. Svarog always takes all factors into account and handles problems logically... I can't do that myself yet.

t_ch: 唔，所以…我们该拿帕斯卡怎么办呢？
t_en: So, what should we do with Pascal?

t_ch: 将机械单元帕斯卡的核心存储格式化，随后纳入基地网络统一管理。
t_en: It is recommended that Pascal's data be formatted and integrated into the base network.

t_ch: 纳入基地网络后，我将对该个体的核心模组进行全面维护，修复其硬件结构。
t_en: I will maintain the robot's core module and repair its hardware once it has been integrated into the base network.

t_ch: 清除逻辑坏道及信息噪音后，该个体的所有出厂功能有超过<unbreak>97.2%</unbreak>的概率恢复正常。但……
t_en: There is a greater than <unbreak>97.2%</unbreak> chance that this robot will return to normal after the bad sectors and muddled information are removed. However...

t_ch: 但，帕斯卡的情感智能…
t_en: But what about Pascal's emotional intelligence...

t_ch: …格式化完成后，该个体发展出的情感智能将完全消失。
t_en: Once the formatting is complete, the robot's emotional intelligence will vanish.

t_ch: 我觉得这是个好办法。
t_en: That sounds like an excellent plan to me.

t_ch: 但…如果选择了格式化，帕斯卡一定会彻底失去情感智能……
t_en: But, once the formatting is complete, Pascal's emotional intelligence will vanish...

t_ch: …没错。
t_en: ...Correct.

t_ch: 我就知道这里肯定有个「但」…
t_en: I know there has to be a catch...

t_ch: 但…如果选择了格式化，帕斯卡一定会彻底失去情感智能……
t_en: But, once the formatting is complete, Pascal's emotional intelligence will vanish...

t_ch: …没错。
t_en: ...Correct.

t_ch: 没有别的办法了吗……
t_en: Is there no other option...?

t_ch: 该方案可以将对内和对外的损失皆控制在最小幅度。尽管根据克拉拉的描述，该个体的状况暂且稳定，但根据演算……
t_en: This option minimizes both internal and external losses. Although Clara's account indicates that the robot is relatively stable, the assessment based on the computations shows that...

t_ch: 机械单元帕斯卡的故障概率及信息噪音比例仍在不断上升。一旦负荷率达到<unbreak>100%</unbreak>——
t_en: Both the risk of Pascal crashing and the amount of muddled information are increasing. When it approaches <unbreak>100%</unbreak>...

t_ch: …克拉拉明白。
t_en: I understand.

t_ch: 但是…克拉拉觉得还不能放弃修复帕斯卡的希望。
t_en: But I don't think I'm ready to give up on Pascal just yet.

t_ch: 我们已经帮帕斯卡找到了兼容的硬件…如果能用其他方法清除信息噪音，不损伤到关联情感智能的扇区……
t_en: We've helped Pascal find compatible hardware. If we can eliminate the muddled information without damaging the sectors linked to his emotional intelligence...

t_ch: ——你知道这种可能性十分微小，克拉拉。
t_en: You know the chances of that are incredibly low, Clara.

t_ch: 史瓦罗的计算这次可能很准确…
t_en: Svarog's calculations are extremely precise.

t_ch: 不，不能就这样放弃…一定、一定还有办法……
t_en: No, I can't just give up. There has to be another way!

t_ch: 你们平时如何清除信息噪音？
t_en: How do you generally get rid of muddled information?

t_ch: 能彻底清除噪音的方法，有且仅有一种：「覆写」。
t_en: There's only one way to completely remove muddled information — overwriting.

t_ch: 基地网络会将扇区对应储存的信息完整备份，并在清除信息噪音时将完整信息覆写至对应区域。
t_en: When the muddled information is removed, the base network will create a comprehensive backup and overwrite the data stored in the corresponding sector.

t_ch: 其实…我听不懂…
t_en: In all honesty, I don't understand...

t_ch: 没关系，办法由克拉拉和史瓦罗先生一起想就好……
t_en: It's okay, Mr. Svarog and I can come up with a solution.

t_ch: …或许还有一种办法。
t_en: ...Maybe there's another way.

t_ch: ——我们…可以在帕斯卡的核心中加一道「锁」。
t_en: We can install a "lock" in Pascal's core.

t_ch: 清除多余的信息，再找到核心中还完好的逻辑轨道…然后植入一套新的运行程序。
t_en: Clear the redundant data and locate the core sectors that are still intact. Then, we can install a new suite of operational programs.

t_ch: 这套程序可以限制帕斯卡的行为，防止它对其他人造成伤害——
t_en: This suite of programs can regulate Pascal's behavior and prevent him from hurting people.

t_ch: 这样的话，应该可以最大程度地保留帕斯卡习得的情感智能，也能避免它继续闯祸……
t_en: With this method, we might be able to retain as much of Pascal's emotional intelligence as possible while keeping him out of trouble...

t_ch: 如果能够保住帕斯卡获得的情感智能…克拉拉或许有办法引导它，让它学会用善意对待他人。史瓦罗先生…你觉得呢？
t_en: If we can keep Pascal's emotional intelligence, I might be able to teach him to treat people with kindness. What do you think, Mr. Svarog?

t_ch: 可以尝试，但有风险——该方法无法根除持续产生的信息噪音。同时，新写入的程序有被噪音污染的风险。
t_en: This procedure is theoretically viable, but there are risks. It will not eliminate the muddled information. Additionally, the newly installed programs may be later contaminated by it.

t_ch: 换言之，考虑到我们无法对网络外的个体进行持续监测，该方法只能在短时间内保证机械单元帕斯卡的稳定性。
t_en: In other words, because we cannot monitor the robot outside of the network, this approach can only guarantee Pascal's stability for a brief period of time.

t_ch: 根据演算，该个体再度发生故障的概率约为……
t_en: The probability of the robot crashing again is around...

t_ch: …到那时候，克拉拉就再重复一遍同样的做法。如果之后再发生…那就再来一次。克拉拉会引导它、修复它——不管重复多少遍，克拉拉都会陪伴、帮助帕斯卡……
t_en: And if that happens, I'll do it again. And if it happens again, I'll go through the exact same steps. I'll guide him and fix him. No matter how many times it happens, I'll be there to help Pascal...

t_ch: 但它会忘记你，克拉拉。它会一遍又一遍地忘记你，还有它此前习得的一切。一切记忆，一切情感。
t_en: But he will forget about you, Clara. He will repeatedly forget you, as well as all the emotions and memories he has previously accumulated.

t_ch: 在无限的循环中，有限的数据空间迟早会被无穷复制的信息噪音彻底覆写。
t_en: His limited storage capacity will eventually become completely overwritten by infinite copies of muddled information in an everlasting cycle.

t_ch: 这条路比你想象的还要艰难，克拉拉。你确定要这样选择吗？
t_en: Clara, the challenge will be tougher than you can possibly imagine. Are you sure this is the path you want to take?

t_ch: 冷静一下，克拉拉。不必急着给出结论。
t_en: There, there Clara. There's no need to rush to a conclusion.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格命运中的「变量」仍在此处。或许{F#她}{M#他}能替我们给出最合适的解决方案。
t_en: The variable in Belobog's fate is still here. Perhaps {F#she}{M#he} can suggest the best solution.

t_ch: …嗯。我…我相信{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}的判断。
t_en: Okay. I... I trust your judgment.

t_ch: 我们相信你的选择，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: We trust your decision, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: （两人都已经给出了自己的解决方案……）
t_en: (Both have provided their solutions...)

t_ch: （史瓦罗认为有必要对帕斯卡进行格式化，纳入基地网络，但这会使帕斯卡失去来之不易的情感智能。）
t_en: (Svarog thinks that formatting Pascal's data and integrating him into the base's network is necessary, but doing so will deprive Pascal of his special emotional intelligence.)

t_ch: （听起来似乎有点残忍，但这个办法或许确实可以根除帕斯卡带来的安全隐患……）
t_en: (Although it may sound cruel, this approach best prevents Pascal from posing a threat...)

t_ch: （而克拉拉认为可以利用程序限制帕斯卡的行为，在保留情感智能的同时防止它造成损害。但这个方法治标不治本，更需要克拉拉付出大量心血……）
t_en: (Clara thinks she can use programming to control Pascal's behavior and prevent him from causing harm while keeping his emotional intelligence intact. However, this doesn't address the root of the issue, and it also requires Clara to devote much time and effort to Pascal.)
